rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIKE ANGEL LEON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To (1) Compel Millie Yang?S Production, Without Objection To Subpoena (Duces Tecum); And (2) Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Nicole C. Kelly In Support With Pos
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Administrator's Motion to Compel Production, Without Objection to Subpoena (Duces Tecum) is GRANTED. While there are some procedural deficiencies, the infirmities are excusable and, in any event, no demonstration of prejudice to the opposition or the witness Millie Yang, CPA, has been shown. Furthermore, no procedural objections have been raised, and pursuant to CCP Section 1987.1(a), the Court may direct compliance with a subpoena on its own motion. CCP Section 1987.2 allows sanctions incurred in making or opposing a motion, including reasonable attorney's fees, if the court finds the motion was made or opposed in bad faith or without substantial justification. CCP Section 2025.480 provides that the court shall impose a monetary sanction against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. The Court finds that the opposition brought by Thomas Smurro as counsel for the San Francisco Eagle Bar, LLC ("LLC"), is in bad faith. Pursuant to Corporations Code Sections 17704.10, 17705.02, and 17705.04 the Administrator may exercise all of the member's rights, including the right to obtain financial information (Corporations Code Section 17702.13(d)) at the LLC's expense. That is, the LLC has a legal obligation to provide the Administrator, as a 50% member in the LLC, with the financial documents requested in the subpoena. Despite that legal obligation, and despite the fact that this motion to compel was brought against Millie Yang, CPA, not the LLC, Attorney Smurro filed an objection; forcing the Administrator to incur additional fees to reply to the opposition and appear at the hearing. The evidence is insufficient to determine that Attorney Smurro's actions caused the fees incurred to draft and file the motion and accompanying pleadings. However, the fees incurred in drafting a reply to the opposition filed by Attorney Smurro and the fees incurred in appearing at the hearing are a direct result of the bad faith opposition. Attorney Smurro has unsuccessfully opposed this motion to compel. Sanctions are awarded to the Administrator in the amount of $1,050 if no appearance is necessary and $1,750 if an appearance is necessary. Attorney Thomas Smurro is ordered to pay such santions to the Administrator within ten days of date of this order. The Administrator shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
